CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-31-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Noticed Motion To Reopen Discovery To Allow For Deposition Of Defendants
Off Calendar Apr-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jennifer Wang (jennifer@summacounseling.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Dianne Peebles (Dianne.Peebles@farmersinsurance.com) of Stratman, Schwartz & Williams-Abrego for cross-defendants Jennifer Want and Nipun Jethi.
Robert E. Camors, Jr. (bobcamors@camorslaw.com) for Defendants.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER WANG AND NIPUN JETHI's Motion To Reopen Discovery To Allow For Deposition Of Defendants:

CCP section 2024.020 provides that a trial continuance ordinarily does not defer discovery cutoff dates. The court's February 5, 2021 order continuing the trial date did not modify any discovery deadlines. However, CCP section 2024.50 provides that discovery may be reopened in the court's discretion, such discretion to be exercised based on the factors enumerated in Section 2024.50. Defendants propose that if the court concludes that the time should be extended for the completion of depositions of the parties, that the depositions of the plaintiffs as well as of the defendants would be warranted, and in their reply brief, plaintiffs state they have no objection to this. The court agrees. In considering the factors enumerated in Section 2024.50, the court finds that in this case, depositions of the parties before trial would be in the interests of judicial economy and in the interests of fairness.

The evidence submitted on this motion shows there was some confusion about what the discovery cutoff date would be, and that, following meet and confer efforts, plaintiffs promptly filed this motion. There has been no showing of prejudice, and no showing that allowing the depositions of the four parties involved to proceed would delay the June 1, 2021 trial date. This matter has proceeded expeditiously since the complaint was filed despite the pandemic. Accordingly, good cause having been shown, plaintiffs may take the depositions of the two defendants, and the defendants may take the depositions of the two plaintiffs, such depositions to be completed by May 11, 2021.

Discovery is open until May 11, 2021 for whatever purposes.

Defendants' counsel is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sigrid.irias@gmail.com cc'ing discovery302@sftc.org and all parties.

Judge Pro Tem: Sigrid Irias, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
